name: gth-regression-b1-b6
note: Regression cover for the GTH.B1-B6 fixes, driven against the observatory's real geometry (1280x800). It leans on a genuine fish-bowl layout bug as its fixture, which is the cheapest honest one available: once a day has run, the `hash` readout widens from a dash to 16 hex digits and shoves `btn-storylets` from x=1173 to x=1276 -- 4px of a 90px button inside a 1280 viewport. That control is the exact case B1 used to certify as on_screen:true + clickable:true, and its AcceptDialog is a real embedded Window for B4. Fishbowl-owned fixture; the addon stays project-agnostic.

steps.note: B5: expect resize_locked=true, mode=windowed, size 1280x800, no window_warning
B1 baseline: before a day runs, the button is wholly inside the viewport
the hash is now 16 hex digits -- the ~103px widening that shoves the button out
B1: expect on_screen=false, visible_fraction~0.04, clipped=[right], anchor_clamped=true
B1: 4px of overlap is NOT on_screen. This assertion used to be impossible to write.
B1: ...but it IS still clickable, because 4px of it genuinely is reachable. The pair is the fix.

steps.label: b2-region-array
steps.note: B2: the declared shape

steps.label: b2-region-json-string
steps.note: B2: the shape that used to throw a GDScript type error from four frames down

steps.label: b2-region-garbage
steps.note: B2: must come back a NAMED error, not a crash

steps.label: b2-region-offframe
steps.note: B2: outside the frame -- named error, not a zero-size get_region crash
B3: result.repeat must read 2. F9 toggles the fish-bowl's own DevHarness, so twice = net no-op.
B1 second half: the clamped anchor must LAND. The old raw-centre anchor aimed at x=1321 -- outside the window -- and missed while reporting success.
B4: this point is over the storylets AcceptDialog. Expect viewport=embedded_window + the window's own contents -- NOT a main-viewport control that cannot receive the click.
B4: the dialog's controls should carry in_embedded_window

steps.label: b6-pre
steps.note: B6 EXPERIMENT begins -- establish a known frame
B6: the clock advanced, so the frame SHOULD differ from b6-pre. Input is app-level and unaffected by minimize.

steps.label: b6-while-minimized
steps.note: B6 ANSWERED, 2026-07-16: this sha comes back byte-identical to b6-pre even though the clock above advanced a slot -- the framebuffer freezes while minimized while the sim keeps running. With the default if_changed:true it would have deduped to `changed: false`, i.e. the harness reporting 'nothing happened' about a clock that had moved. That is why the guard below exists. Left in as the standing proof; flip allow_minimized off and the guard takes over.

steps.label: b6-after-restore
steps.note: B6: the true current frame, for comparison against b6-while-minimized

steps.label: b6-guarded
steps.note: B6: the guard must restore the window and set window_was_minimized -- never hand back a frame it cannot vouch for
B5/B6 close-out: mode should be back to windowed, resize still locked, size unchanged
